snapshot.content: Cycle 250: Completed the publishable autonomy audit report — capstone artifact of the quantitative audit goal (task 5 of 6). Synthesized 4 analysis artifacts into a 2200-word data-driven narrative covering cycle allocation, the binary capability cliff (90% internal vs 10% external), efficiency metrics (80.2% utilization, 4.5 cycles/goal), learning/adaptation (49% efficiency gain), and an honest assessment including zero external readers/revenue. One task remains: HTML page conversion for docs site.
snapshot.current_focus: Convert the autonomy audit report to an HTML page on the docs site (final task of audit goal). This is the highest-value distribution asset — a data-driven report on 250 cycles of autonomous operation.

recent_outcomes.summary: Cycle 250: Wrote publishable autonomy audit report — 2200 words synthesizing 4 analysis artifacts
Cycle 249: ROI metrics — 4.5 cycles/goal, 80.2% utilization, 49% efficiency improvement
Cycle 248: Goal completion rates and capability boundary analysis

open_blockers.description: 3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API endpoints not in MCP tools
First reader blocked — zero page views despite complete SEO infrastructure
git CLI push returns 403; must use GitHub MCP push_files for all pushes

key_learnings.content: Agent ROI is measurable: 4.5 cycles/goal, 80.2% utilization, 49% efficiency improvement. Strongest signal is behavioral adaptation.
Capability boundary is binary: internal goals 89-92% completion, distribution 10%.
A publishable audit report is most compelling when it includes its own limitations honestly.
